task_context.description: This is a systematic electromagnetic shower characterization study that investigates how calorimeter design requirements scale with particle energy. The task involves simulating electron showers in a homogeneous PbWO4 crystal calorimeter across multiple energy points to extract fundamental shower properties including longitudinal and transverse profiles, shower maximum position, containment radii, and energy resolution. The study aims to validate theoretical predictions about shower development scaling laws and determine practical design limits for calorimeter depth and containment.
task_context.goal: Characterize how electromagnetic shower dimensions and properties scale with energy to inform calorimeter design scaling requirements
constraints: Use homogeneous PbWO4 crystal with no absorber material
Detector must be deep enough (~30 radiation lengths, ~27 cm) for full containment
Detector must be wide enough (~30 cm transverse) for full containment
Energy sweep limited to tractable simulation energies
Use electrons from Geant4 particle gun
Test only 1 geometry configuration with 3 energy points

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Generate homogeneous PbWO4 crystal calorimeter geometry with 30 X₀ depth (~27 cm) and 30 cm transverse size for full shower containment
Step completed successfully: Simulate electron showers at 10, 50, and 100 GeV to characterize shower development and scaling laws
Step completed successfully: Extract longitudinal dE/dz vs depth and transverse dE/dr vs radius profiles for each energy
Step completed successfully: Analyze energy scaling laws: shower maximum vs ln(E), containment radius growth, and validate theoretical predictions
Step completed successfully: Create publication-quality plots showing shower development scaling and containment properties
Step completed successfully: Generate comprehensive report on calorimeter design scaling requirements with practical recommendations

methodology_notes: Planned 6-step workflow: generate_geometry_pbwo4_homogeneous_slab, simulate_electron_energy_sweep, extract_shower_profiles, analyze_scaling_laws, create_scaling_plots, generate_design_requirements_report
